slides.title: The Sorting Game
slides.text:
Filling a Venn Diagram is just like sorting objects into baskets.

Let's sort shapes based on two rules:
Set A: Must be RED.
Set B: Must be a CIRCLE.

slides.title: Rule 1: The Middle First!
slides.text:
Pro Tip: Always fill the Intersection first!

Ask: "Is there anything that is BOTH Red AND a Circle?"

Yes! The Red Circle (🔴) goes in the center.

slides.title: Rule 2: Fill the Sides
slides.text:
Now, place the rest:

1. Red Square (🟥): It is Red, but NOT a circle. It goes to the Left (Red Only).

2. Blue Circle (🔵): It is a Circle, but NOT red. It goes to the Right (Circle Only).

slides.title: Rule 3: The Outsiders
slides.text:
What about a Blue Square (🟦)?

It is NOT red. It is NOT a circle.
It gets kicked outside the rings, but stays inside the box (Universal Set).

slides.title: Applying to Numbers
slides.text:
Now let's try Math!
Set A = Factors of 6: {1, 2, 3, 6}
Set B = Factors of 9: {1, 3, 9}

Step 1: Find the Middle (Common members).
Both have 1 and 3.

slides.title: Calculating 'Only'
slides.text:
Step 2: Fill the rest.

Set A (Left): We used {1,3}. Remaining: {2, 6}.
Set B (Right): We used {1,3}. Remaining: {9}.

Notice: We don't write {1,3} again!
